quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: Wave Graphs and Calculations
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question:
A displacement-time graph of a wave is shown below.

How many complete wave cycles were completed in $1\text{ s}$?
questions.explanation: From the graph, one cycle takes $0.4\text{ s}$. In $1\text{ s}$, the number of cycles $= 1/0.4 = 2.5$.

questions.question:
A displacement-time graph of a wave is shown below.

What is the frequency of the wave?
A displacement-time graph of a wave is shown below. If this wave travels through a medium at a constant speed of $20.0\text{ m/s}$, what is its wavelength?
questions.explanation: From the graph, $T = 0.4\text{ s}$, so $f = 2.5\text{ Hz}$. $\lambda = v/f = 20.0/2.5 = 8.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question:
A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below.

What is the wavelength of this wave?
questions.explanation: The wavelength is the distance over which the wave repeats. One complete cycle (crest and trough) spans exactly $1.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question:
A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below.

What is the amplitude of this wave?
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the maximum displacement from equilibrium. The crest reaches $+4.0\text{ m}$ and the trough reaches $-4.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question:
A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below.

How many complete wave cycles are captured within the total $3.0\text{ m}$ distance shown on the graph?
questions.explanation: Each cycle occupies $1.0\text{ m}$. Across $3.0\text{ m}$, the wave completes $3.0/1.0 = 3.0$ cycles.

questions.question:
A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below.

What is the total vertical distance between a crest and a trough for this wave?
questions.explanation: Crest to trough distance equals twice the amplitude. $2 \times 4.0\text{ m} = 8.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question: A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below. If the frequency of this wave is $5.0\text{ Hz}$, what is the speed of the wave?
questions.explanation: $\lambda = 1.0\text{ m}$ from the graph. $v = f\lambda = 5.0 \times 1.0 = 5.0\text{ m/s}$.

questions.question: A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below. If the wave travels at a constant speed of $12.0\text{ m/s}$, what is the period of the wave?

A displacement-distance graph of a wave is shown below.

Which pair of distance coordinates represent two positions that are completely out of phase with each other?
questions.explanation: Points out of phase are separated by $\lambda/2 = 0.5\text{ m}$. At $0.25\text{ m}$ the wave is at a crest, and at $0.75\text{ m}$ it is at a trough — completely out of phase.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered reading and interpreting wave graphs!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the difference between displacement-time and displacement-distance graphs.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on extracting period from time graphs and wavelength from distance graphs.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: identifying amplitude, period, wavelength, and frequency from wave graphs.
